steps: $L(p,q)$ is a closed orientable 3-manifold, so $H_3 = \mathbb{Z}$ (fundamental class) and $H_0 = \mathbb{Z}$ (connected).
The CW structure has one cell in each dimension $0, 1, 2, 3$. The boundary maps are: $d_1 = 0$ (the 1-cell is a loop generating $\pi_1 = \mathbb{Z}/p$), $d_2 = \times p$, $d_3 = 0$.
Note: $H_*(L(p,q))$ depends only on $p$, not on $q$. To distinguish $L(p,q)$ from $L(p,q')$ one needs finer invariants (linking form, Reidemeister torsion).
